name: scaffold
context: Generate a new Dojo node directory with skeleton node.json, SKILL.md, and scripts from a template
info: The scaffold sub-skill creates a complete node directory structure from a template. Given a URI, type, and optional context, it generates the directory tree, writes a skeleton node.json with all required fields pre-filled, creates a SKILL.md for executable nodes, and stubs out a scripts/ directory. The skeleton is immediately valid JSON that passes schema validation — the author's job is to enrich it with knowledge content. Templates exist for each of the five node types: ecosystem, standard, skill, context, and sub. See [[dojo/templates]] for the full template catalog.
triggers: create a node from a template
body:
# Scaffold a Node

Scaffolding creates the minimum viable node — a valid directory structure with a skeleton `node.json` that passes schema validation. The author then enriches it with real knowledge.

## Usage

```bash
# Scaffold an ecosystem node
./scripts/scaffold-node.sh ecosystem redis

# Scaffold a skill node under an existing parent
./scripts/scaffold-node.sh skill github/actions/workflows

# Scaffold a context node
./scripts/scaffold-node.sh context kubernetes/troubleshooting
```

## What Gets Created

For a skill node at `github/actions/workflows`:

```
nodes/github/actions/workflows/
├── node.json           # skeleton with all required fields
├── SKILL.md            # Agent Skills frontmatter + placeholder body
└── scripts/            # empty scripts directory
```

For a context node at `kubernetes/troubleshooting`:

```
nodes/kubernetes/troubleshooting/
└── node.json           # skeleton with required + knowledge fields
```

## Skeleton node.json

The generated `node.json` includes:

```json
{
  "name": "workflows",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "uri": "github/actions/workflows",
  "type": "skill",
  "context": "TODO: one-line description under 200 chars",
  "info": "TODO: dense paragraph with scope, surface, and why it matters",
  "parent": "github/actions",
  "tags": ["workflows"],
  "aliases": [],
  "triggers": [],
  "body": "",
  "sections": [],
  "links": [],
  "related": [],
  "author": "dojo-community",
  "license": "MIT",
  "created": "2026-04-11T00:00:00Z",
  "updated": "2026-04-11T00:00:00Z",
  "status": "draft"
}
```

## Next Steps

After scaffolding:
1. Fill in `context` and `info` with real content
2. Add `aliases` and `triggers` for discovery
3. Write `body` with explanations, code examples, and `[[wiki-links]]`
4. Add `sections` for addressable knowledge chunks
5. Add `links` and `related` for graph connections
6. For skill/sub: add `scripts`, `schema`, and `depends`
7. Run [[dojo/validate]] to check completeness

Or use [[dojo/authoring/enrich]] to automate knowledge enrichment.

sections.title: Usage
sections.body: Run `./scripts/scaffold-node.sh <type> <uri>` where type is one of ecosystem, standard, skill, context, sub. The URI is the full slash-separated path. The script auto-detects the parent from the URI, creates the directory under `nodes/`, and writes the skeleton files. Pass `--with-scripts` to also create a `scripts/` directory with a stub script file.

sections.title: Template variants
sections.body: Five templates exist, one per node type. Ecosystem templates include `more` links. Skill templates include `scripts`, `schema`, and `depends` placeholders. Context templates include `content_type` and `frontmatter` with reading-time estimates. Sub templates inherit the parent's tags. All templates generate valid JSON that passes [[dojo/validate/schema]]. See [[dojo/templates]] for the full catalog.

links.context: Enrich scaffolded nodes with deep knowledge
Full template catalog for all node types
Manifest contract the scaffold must satisfy

related.note: Scaffolding is a sub-operation of the authoring workflow
Enrich runs after scaffold to add knowledge depth
Scaffold reads from the templates catalog

scripts.name: Scaffold Node
scripts.description: Generate a new node directory with skeleton node.json and optional SKILL.md from a type template

depends.reason: Scaffold reads skeleton structures from the templates node

author: dojo-community
